# Desktop apps, reimagined by you.

Create any app in minutes by chatting with AI. Beautiful, powerful, and truly personal.

Download for Mac

macOS Tahoe and Apple Silicon required

Play

Available now for macOS Tahoe and Apple Silicon

Local-firstApps run on your machine, no server or internet connection required.

OS-integratedAccess files, tools, and anything on your operating system.

OpinionatedBeautiful by default and personal when you want it to be.

## Make every idea an app

Describe it and watch it take shape right where you work.

## Publish effortlessly

Easily share with your team or release publicly.

## Made for teams

Internal software built around your tools and processes.

## Discover & install [...] ## Discover & install

Explore apps from your team and our wider community of builders.

## FAQ

Glaze lets you build your own desktop apps just by describing what you want. They live on your Mac, launch instantly, and work offline, so you get real software that fits the way you work.

Download Glaze for Mac and start building. Glaze currently requires macOS Tahoe and Apple Silicon, with Windows and Linux support planned down the road.

No. You describe what you want in plain language and Glaze builds it. If something isn’t right, just talk to it and change it. And if you do know how to code, you’ll feel right at home shaping things further. [...] Yes! You own your app, its code and content.

You can share an app with anyone, they just need Glaze installed and to be signed in to their account. They don't have to be a paid subscriber to run it. For private sharing, the Pro plan lets you publish 'Unlisted' apps for 1:1 sharing, and the Team plan gives you a private store for your team. You can also publish to the public Glaze Store, where anyone can discover, download and use your app.

Yes. Glaze apps run on your Mac, so they can connect to your APIs, local files, and hardware. You can hook into the tools you already use, pull in AI models, or connect to any service with an API.

Mac to start. Glaze currently requires macOS Tahoe and Apple Silicon. Windows and Linux will come down the road.

# Introducing Glaze

Create beautiful desktop apps in minutes by chatting with AI. Now in private beta.

Avatar

Avatar

It used to be hard to make software. It took time, money, and the right talent. So a small number of companies built apps for millions of people, and everyone got the same thing.

That's changing. Anyone can create software now. But that shouldn't mean settling for something that feels worse to use. We think you deserve apps that are genuinely beautiful, deeply capable, and truly personal.

That's Glaze. Our take on creating desktop apps by chatting with AI. Apps that look, feel, and run like they belong on your Mac.

## Real Desktop Apps, Not Websites [...] ## Real Desktop Apps, Not Websites

Tell Glaze what you want and it’ll build a real desktop app that lives on your computer, launches instantly, and works offline. Because it runs on your machine, it supports keyboard shortcuts, menu bar integration, file system access, and background processes. Every app is beautiful by default and personal when you want it to be. And if something isn't right, just talk to it and change it.

Launch dashboards, music players, and more. All real apps built with Glaze. [...] Join the waitlist

We're opening the private beta for Glaze today, March 4th. Mac only to start. Glaze apps will be deeply integrated with the new version of Raycast that we'll release in April. Existing Raycast users will get priority access to Glaze soon.

Stop searching for the perfect app. Start making it.

# First Look at Glaze: A new Product by Raycast
## Raycast
48700 subscribers
3242 likes

### Description
64686 views
Posted: 4 Mar 2026
Today we're launching a new product: Glaze.

With Glaze, you can build any desktop app in minutes by chatting with AI. Beautiful, powerful, and truly personal.

Learn more about Glaze 👉 
Subscribe to Glaze's channel 👉 @glazeapp 
Read the announcement post 👉 
Watch the ad 👉 [...] apps. And we've been leveraging this ourselves. Peter built this Raycast dashboard which helps us monitor our core metrics and analytics such as our active users on macOS, Windows, and iOS and our social channels such as YouTube, Twitter, and Reddit. It's worth highlighting that one, these numbers are not real and two, how quickly everything loads. The data is saved directly on your device so you don't have to wait for it to fetch everything again. I really can't stress enough how convenient it is to be able to create fully fledged desktop apps like this with Glaze. Raycast has always been praised as one of the best Mac apps out there. Our team of designers and engineers have insane attention to detail and care a lot about shipping the best quality product. With Glaze, you get to make [...] rich text editor and a super plain one. But, let's not stop there. Let's add one more thing. Because I have Ollama installed locally, I'm going to ask Glaze to add a button to auto-generate the title using Ollama. So, here we have it. Um I should be able to click on this button now to generate a new title. And just like that, I've built my own note-taking desktop app with local notes, markdown support, and even integrated a local AI model. I could go on and on, but I think you get the gist. It's really fun to create with Glaze, and I've spent hours iterating on my own apps this past few weeks, and so has the Raycast team. And because of that, our creations have had time to mature. They've been tested by teammates, iterated on. So, I wanted to showcase some of the really cool things that

results.title: Glaze - Build Mac Apps Without Coding - Wonder Tools
results.content:
Include options for your apps. Instruct Glaze to give anyone using your app choices. In my QuotePop app, for example, you can pick your image dimensions, background color, and font.

Plan first. The clearer you are about what you want before Glaze starts building, the fewer credits you’ll burn on rework. Put Glaze in planning mode to start. Or, if you already pay for another AI tool like Claude or ChatGPT, plan there and give Glaze a summary. That saves your Glaze credits for building.

Iterate to improve. Once Glaze builds the first version of your app, give it a list of fixes to improve the app’s design or make it easier to use.

## Limitations

Mac only for now. No specific timeline for Windows and Linux. [...] Wonder Tools

# Wonder Tools

# Make Your Own Apps 🚀

### Glaze: New Vibe Coding for Beginners

Jeremy Caplan's avatar

Glaze is a new Mac app for making your own software. It’s for vibe coding, meaning you just describe any kind of tool, game, or app you want to create. Glaze builds it. It’s like having a friend who codes and is happy to add whatever small feature you ask for.

Share

How Glaze is Different: Unlike other vibe coding tools, like Lovable, Bolt, Gemini Canvas, Google’s AI Studio, or Claude Artifacts, Glaze creates software that runs locally on your computer, not on the web. That means:

Your apps works offline

Your data stays on your machine [...] A digital illustration of the Glaze app-building agent. At the top, a 'Genmoji' project window shows AI processes like 'Project set-up' and 'Planning.' Below, a gallery of colorful, stylized app icons sits above the headline 'Build any desktop app,' illustrating how users can generate software from descriptions. - Alt-text drafted with Gemini assistance. The gif shows 3 images in total. One for building apps, another for publishing apps, and one for discovering apps you can install.

## Pro Tips

Customize Glaze’s Instructions. Summarize your preferences in Glaze’s settings (screenshot). That way your apps will have your preferred design elements or features.
